decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.29°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective and conservative first step is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader material to enhance lateral heat spreading, reducing the local hot spot intensity.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 1.5x improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly addressing the high severity temperature violation while staying within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity is increased further without geometric changes
Higher conductivity materials may have cost or manufacturing implications not modeled here
